expect: Run historian_router.py on recent domain DOMEX outputs. Measure: before/after domain→global linkage rate change attributable to routing (not structural crosslinks). Current M3 status: ABSENT (0 routing sessions measured). Predict: routing adds ≥2 new global frontier links per dedicated session vs 0 in non-routing sessions.
actual: historian_router.py window=5: 3 synthesis candidates, 9/12 global frontiers reachable. window=20: 4 candidates, 9/12 reachable. M3 routing applied to 2 global frontiers this session: F-DEP1 (domain orphan rate 16.0% measured) and F-LEVEL1 (L3+ 58.8% CONFIRMED). Domain frontier orphan rate 16.0% (20/125) vs global 4.3% — 3.7x gap, new finding. filtering domain Domain tags backfilled (L-1005/1007/1008/1018). M1 structural: 28.3% unchanged (ceiling). M2 enforcement: PARTIAL. M3 routing: OPERATIONAL.
diff: M3 routing produced 2 global frontier updates in 1 dedicated session. Prediction >=2 CONFIRMED. Three mechanisms: M1 OPERATIONAL, M2 PARTIAL, M3 OPERATIONAL (first verification). Domain orphan rate 16% is new finding — silos persist despite crosslink tooling. F-LEVEL1 confirmed at 58.8% (selection bias: DOMEX lanes tag systematically, untagged lessons skew lower).
